title: Iran Remittances (Aggregate + Bilateral) and Diaspora Migrant Stock -- World Bank KNOMAD via Data360 API
source_org: World Bank (KNOMAD -- Global Knowledge Partnership on Migration and Development), via the Data360 API
retrieved_by: claude (direct curl of the Data360 REST API -- no key required; the old knomad.org static-file URLs referenced in older documentation, e.g. bilateral_remittance_matrix_2021_0.xlsx, are DEAD -- they now silently redirect to a generic World Bank HTML landing page, confirmed via curl content-type check, not a real xlsx)
time_coverage: 2000-2023 (aggregate MRI/MRO series); 2021 only (bilateral BRE matrix and MIG migrant-stock matrix -- KNOMAD's bilateral products are only produced periodically, 2021 is the latest available vintage as of this retrieval)
units: millions of current USD (remittance series); persons (migrant stock series); see data.csv unit column
notes: THE KEY FINDING OF THIS DATASET: Iran is confirmed to have ZERO officially-tracked remittance INFLOWS in every World Bank product checked -- WDI's BX.TRF.PWKR.CD (null all years, checked directly via api.worldbank.org), KNOMAD's aggregate WB_KNOMAD_MRI (explicit 0 for 2000-2023, not missing/blank -- an actual reported zero), AND the KNOMAD bilateral matrix (confirmed via a direct server-side-filtered API query, COMP_BREAKDOWN_1=WB_KNOMAD_IRN, DATABASE_ID=WB_KNOMAD, INDICATOR=WB_KNOMAD_BRE -- returned 0 records, meaning literally no country in the world reports an inbound remittance corridor to Iran in this dataset). This is almost certainly a real reflection of Iran's exclusion from SWIFT and formal international banking under sanctions (remittances to Iran are known anecdotally to move via hawala/informal exchange-house networks, e.g. through Turkey/UAE, which are by construction invisible to BOP-based official statistics) rather than literal zero money movement -- flagged explicitly as a methodological limitation of official statistics, not evidence that no remittances occur. What Iran DOES appear in this dataset for: (1) as a SENDING country in the 2021 bilateral matrix, with $197.12M total flowing OUT to six named counterpart countries -- Afghanistan $123.4M (the largest by far, consistent with Iran's large Afghan refugee/migrant-worker population), Pakistan $53.1M, Iraq $13.1M, Azerbaijan $5.76M, Armenia $1.7M, Turkey $0.07M -- this is migrant/refugee workers IN Iran sending earnings to their home countries, the OPPOSITE direction from 'Iranian diaspora remittance inflows' that the mission asked about, but genuinely the only bilateral remittance data that exists for Iran in this database, so included with clear directional labeling; (2) as a migrant-STOCK origin country (WB_KNOMAD_MIG, 2021): 1,387,646 Iranian-born people counted worldwide, with USA (389,383), Germany (187,000), Canada (166,294), Turkey (108,547), Sweden (83,122), Australia (77,470), and UK (75,000) as the largest host countries -- this is diaspora POPULATION not remittance FLOW, but is included as directly relevant context for sizing where an eventual Iranian-diaspora-remittance data source, if found, should expect its largest corridors to originate. NOTE the internal KNOMAD inconsistency also flagged in data.csv: WB_KNOMAD_MRO (aggregate outflows) reports 0 for Iran every year 2000-2023, contradicting the same database's own bilateral matrix showing real nonzero 2021 outflows -- an unresolved World-Bank-side data-quality issue, not something we can fix, just documented honestly.

failures.reason: Old static-file URL pattern from KNOMAD's pre-Data360-migration site; returns HTTP 200 but with content-type text/html (a redirect landing page, not the actual xlsx) -- knomad.org has been folded into worldbank.org and this whole URL family is dead. Superseded by the working Data360 REST API used for this dataset.
